name: anyio
summary: High-level concurrency and networking framework on top of asyncio or Trio

story.problem: Python has two competing async runtimes: asyncio (built-in) and trio (third-party). Libraries that hardcode asyncio can't be used in trio applications and vice versa. Framework authors had to pick one and exclude the other. anyio provides a compatibility layer so library authors can write async code once and have it work on both backends.
story.mental_model: anyio is an abstraction over async primitives — task groups, cancellation, sockets, file I/O, locks. When you import from anyio, you get APIs that run correctly whether the underlying runtime is asyncio or trio. Applications choose the backend at startup; libraries built on anyio just work on either.
story.when_to_use: Use anyio when writing an async library or framework that you want to be runtime-agnostic. It's also useful in applications that want trio's structured concurrency model but need to interop with asyncio-based libraries like FastAPI or aiohttp.
story.when_not_to_use: For application code that commits to asyncio (e.g. a FastAPI app), using asyncio directly is simpler — there's no need for the compatibility layer. anyio shines at the library level, not the application level.

alternatives.name: asyncio
alternatives.reason: Python's built-in async runtime — sufficient for applications that don't need trio compatibility

alternatives.name: trio
alternatives.reason: Alternative async runtime with a cleaner structured concurrency model; use anyio on top of it to maintain compatibility

alternatives.name: curio
alternatives.reason: Experimental async library from the asyncio author; mostly superseded by trio and asyncio improvements

nodes.label: anyio
nodes.summary: Async compatibility layer over asyncio and trio. Import as `import anyio`.

nodes.label: anyio.run()
nodes.summary: Entry point: `anyio.run(main)`. Runs on asyncio or trio based on backend param.

nodes.label: anyio.create_task_group()
nodes.summary: Structured concurrency: spawn tasks, auto-cancel siblings on failure. `async with anyio.create_task_group() as tg: tg.start_soon(coro)`.

nodes.label: await anyio.sleep(seconds)
nodes.summary: Async sleep. Use sleep(0) to yield control.

nodes.label: move_on_after / fail_after
nodes.summary: Timeout scopes: move_on_after cancels silently, fail_after raises TimeoutError.

nodes.label: Byte / Object Streams
nodes.summary: anyio.create_memory_object_stream() for in-process producer/consumer. Also TCP/Unix socket streams.

nodes.label: Synchronization Primitives
nodes.summary: Lock, Event, Semaphore, Condition, CapacityLimiter — compatible across asyncio/trio.

nodes.label: anyio.to_thread.run_sync()
nodes.summary: Run blocking code in a thread pool without blocking the event loop.

nodes.label: anyio.from_thread.run()
nodes.summary: Call async code from a sync thread (started by to_thread.run_sync).
